lines.line: # Introduction to the Interview

> The speaker, Grace Midlothian, introduces herself as a state historian from the College of Idaho and outlines the purpose of the interview with Scott Gibson, president of a printing company. The interview is supported by the NEA Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program and aims to explore the influence of a family-run business on careers, relationships, and community growth through adaptation.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, Grace Midlothian, introduces herself as a state historian from the College of Idaho and outlines the purpose of the interview with Scott Gibson, president of a printing company. The interview is supported by the NEA Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program and aims to explore the influence of a family-run business on careers, relationships, and community growth through adaptation.

lines.line: # Early Life and Education

> The speaker recounts his early life in Caldwell, Idaho, where his family has been established since 1895. He shares his educational journey through Caldwell schools, the University of Idaho, and law school, culminating in his decision not to practice law and return to the family business.

metadata.title: Early Life and Education
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts his early life in Caldwell, Idaho, where his family has been established since 1895. He shares his educational journey through Caldwell schools, the University of Idaho, and law school, culminating in his decision not to practice law and return to the family business.

lines.line: # Career Path and Joining the Family Business

> Initially uncertain about his career path, the speaker changed majors several times in college and attended law school due to his strengths in reading and writing. Despite an internship at a law firm, he realized he did not want to practice law and decided to join the family publishing business.

metadata.title: Career Path and Joining the Family Business
metadata.synopsis: Initially uncertain about his career path, the speaker changed majors several times in college and attended law school due to his strengths in reading and writing. Despite an internship at a law firm, he realized he did not want to practice law and decided to join the family publishing business.

lines.line: # Working with Family and Business Adaptation

> The speaker describes the experience of working with his father for over 32 years as an honor, despite the challenges that sometimes arise from working with family. He also discusses the evolution of the family business and his initial reluctance to join it.

metadata.title: Working with Family and Business Adaptation
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes the experience of working with his father for over 32 years as an honor, despite the challenges that sometimes arise from working with family. He also discusses the evolution of the family business and his initial reluctance to join it.

lines.line: # Impact of Law Degree and Business Operations

> The speaker finds his law degree useful in handling contracts, HR issues, and legal matters for the business. He outlines his daily responsibilities as president, which include financial management, communication, and problem-solving, and notes the efficiency gains from technology.

metadata.title: Impact of Law Degree and Business Operations
metadata.synopsis: The speaker finds his law degree useful in handling contracts, HR issues, and legal matters for the business. He outlines his daily responsibilities as president, which include financial management, communication, and problem-solving, and notes the efficiency gains from technology.

lines.line: # Company's Publishing History and Personal Preferences

> The speaker shares the company's history of publishing since 1925 and his personal preference for reading genres different from what the company publishes. He expresses pride in specific publications, such as a book on the Minidoka internment camps and works on the Nez Perce tribe.

metadata.title: Company's Publishing History and Personal Preferences
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares the company's history of publishing since 1925 and his personal preference for reading genres different from what the company publishes. He expresses pride in specific publications, such as a book on the Minidoka internment camps and works on the Nez Perce tribe.

lines.line: # Textbook Distribution and Sales

> The speaker clarifies that while the company does not publish textbooks, it distributes them statewide, handling significant sales volumes annually. He explains the logistics of textbook distribution and its dependency on state education funding.

metadata.title: Textbook Distribution and Sales
metadata.synopsis: The speaker clarifies that while the company does not publish textbooks, it distributes them statewide, handling significant sales volumes annually. He explains the logistics of textbook distribution and its dependency on state education funding.

lines.line: # Company's Evolution and Technological Advancements

> The speaker discusses the company's technological advancements, including the addition of a state-of-the-art digital offset press. He reflects on the company's history, its adaptation to technological changes, and the impact of these advancements on efficiency and employee numbers.

metadata.title: Company's Evolution and Technological Advancements
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the company's technological advancements, including the addition of a state-of-the-art digital offset press. He reflects on the company's history, its adaptation to technological changes, and the impact of these advancements on efficiency and employee numbers.

lines.line: # Community Involvement and Nonprofit Work

> The speaker details his involvement with various nonprofit organizations, such as the Wittenberger Foundation and the YMCA, and their contributions to the community. He emphasizes the importance of giving back to the community and the pride he takes in being part of Caldwell's revitalization.

metadata.title: Community Involvement and Nonprofit Work
metadata.synopsis: The speaker details his involvement with various nonprofit organizations, such as the Wittenberger Foundation and the YMCA, and their contributions to the community. He emphasizes the importance of giving back to the community and the pride he takes in being part of Caldwell's revitalization.

lines.line: # Legacy and Community Impact

> The speaker hopes to leave a legacy of continued business presence and community contribution in Caldwell. He expresses pride in the company's resilience through historical challenges and its role in the community's development, particularly in revitalizing downtown Caldwell.

metadata.title: Legacy and Community Impact
metadata.synopsis: The speaker hopes to leave a legacy of continued business presence and community contribution in Caldwell. He expresses pride in the company's resilience through historical challenges and its role in the community's development, particularly in revitalizing downtown Caldwell.

lines.line: # Personal Reflections and Company's Resilience

> The speaker reflects on the company's impact on the community, emphasizing its historical resilience through wars, economic downturns, and pandemics. He takes pride in the company's ability to navigate these events while maintaining a respected name and contributing to the community's well-being.

metadata.title: Personal Reflections and Company's Resilience
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the company's impact on the community, emphasizing its historical resilience through wars, economic downturns, and pandemics. He takes pride in the company's ability to navigate these events while maintaining a respected name and contributing to the community's well-being.

lines.line: # Focus on Youth and Education

> The speaker explains his focus on youth and education, citing personal enjoyment and the importance of investing in the community's future. He believes that supporting youth leads to a stronger community and encourages the return of talented individuals who can contribute to its growth.

metadata.title: Focus on Youth and Education
metadata.synopsis: The speaker explains his focus on youth and education, citing personal enjoyment and the importance of investing in the community's future. He believes that supporting youth leads to a stronger community and encourages the return of talented individuals who can contribute to its growth.

lines.line: # Publishing Industry Insights

> The speaker shares insights into the publishing industry, including the unexpected resilience of print books against ebooks and the challenges of selling books in an Amazon-dominated market. He also reveals a personal connection to literature through his fondness for 'The Lord of the Rings' trilogy.

metadata.title: Publishing Industry Insights
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares insights into the publishing industry, including the unexpected resilience of print books against ebooks and the challenges of selling books in an Amazon-dominated market. He also reveals a personal connection to literature through his fondness for 'The Lord of the Rings' trilogy.

> The speaker, Grace Midlothian, introduces herself as a state historian from the College of Idaho and outlines the purpose of the interview with Scott Gibson, president of a printing company. The interview is supported by the NEA Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program and aims to explore the influence of a family-run business on careers, relationships, and community growth through adaptation.
# Early Life and Education

> The speaker recounts his early life in Caldwell, Idaho, where his family has been established since 1895. He shares his educational journey through Caldwell schools, the University of Idaho, and law school, culminating in his decision not to practice law and return to the family business.
# Career Path and Joining the Family Business

> Initially uncertain about his career path, the speaker changed majors several times in college and attended law school due to his strengths in reading and writing. Despite an internship at a law firm, he realized he did not want to practice law and decided to join the family publishing business.
# Working with Family and Business Adaptation

> The speaker describes the experience of working with his father for over 32 years as an honor, despite the challenges that sometimes arise from working with family. He also discusses the evolution of the family business and his initial reluctance to join it.
# Impact of Law Degree and Business Operations

> The speaker finds his law degree useful in handling contracts, HR issues, and legal matters for the business. He outlines his daily responsibilities as president, which include financial management, communication, and problem-solving, and notes the efficiency gains from technology.
# Company's Publishing History and Personal Preferences

> The speaker shares the company's history of publishing since 1925 and his personal preference for reading genres different from what the company publishes. He expresses pride in specific publications, such as a book on the Minidoka internment camps and works on the Nez Perce tribe.
# Textbook Distribution and Sales

> The speaker clarifies that while the company does not publish textbooks, it distributes them statewide, handling significant sales volumes annually. He explains the logistics of textbook distribution and its dependency on state education funding.
# Company's Evolution and Technological Advancements

> The speaker discusses the company's technological advancements, including the addition of a state-of-the-art digital offset press. He reflects on the company's history, its adaptation to technological changes, and the impact of these advancements on efficiency and employee numbers.

# Personal Reflections and Company's Resilience

> The speaker shares insights into the publishing industry, including the unexpected resilience of print books against ebooks and the challenges of selling books in an Amazon-dominated market. story.title: Gipson_Scott_OH2812 Hist305
story.description: The recorded interview is with Scott Gipson, president of Caxton Printers in Caldwell, Idaho. Over the conversation, Gipson recounts his personal journey from a history and law education to returning to the family-owned printing and publishing business, emphasizing the challenges and satisfactions of running a long-standing company. He describes the evolution of Caxton Printers, founded in the late 19th century, and its impact on the community, highlighting its role as a textbook distributor and publisher of significant regional nonfiction works. Gipson also discusses the technological advancements in printing and the company's adaptation to the digital age. Community involvement, particularly in youth education and non-profit organizations, is a focal point, reflecting the family's legacy and dedication to Caldwell's development. Gipson's future aspiration is to ensure the company's continued success and community contribution, maintained through familial stewardship across generations.
